channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: February 2026 Sees Lower Unemployment Due to Administrative and Transport Sector Job Gains
item.description: In February 2026, the Philippines saw its unemployment rate decrease to 5.1%, affecting approximately 2.66 million Filipinos, according to the Philippine Statistics Authority. This is an improvement f...

item.title: ASX200 surges as Trump announces ceasefire with Iran
item.description: Australian share markets experienced a significant boost, with gains approaching $80 billion, following the announcement of a ceasefire between the United States and Iran. The ASX200 index, which trac...

item.title: Exploring the Potential of Weight Loss Medications for People Living with HIV in South Africa
item.description: South Africa is facing a growing health challenge as HIV-related weight gain and obesity have become common among people living with HIV. With this, there's growing interest in the use of weight loss ...

item.title: Pakistan mediates two-week ceasefire between US and Iran, aiming for broader peace
item.description: On Wednesday, Pakistan successfully facilitated a significant ceasefire between the United States and Iran, including their allies, effective immediately and covering Lebanon. Prime Minister Shehbaz S...

item.title: Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau's Romance Sparks Debate After Shared Photo
item.description: Pop star Katy Perry and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau have generated significant online discussion after sharing a photograph of themselves looking affectionate. The image, posted on Perry's ...

item.title: TAZARA Railway Celebrates 50 Years, Commits to Revitalization and Continued Partnership
item.description: The Tanzania-Zambia Railway Authority (TAZARA) is marking its 50th anniversary of commercial operations, a significant milestone highlighting a legacy of sacrifice, collaboration, and enduring partner...

item.title: Telangana Government to Promote Normal Deliveries Through Expanded Midwifery Training in Nursing Colleges
item.description: On April 8, 2026, Telangana Health Minister C. Damodar Raja Narasimha announced that the State government will roll out midwifery training programmes across all government nursing colleges. The initia...

item.title: Congress criticises Modi’s diplomacy after Pakistan mediates US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: The Congress party has accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of a 'severe setback' in his foreign policy after Pakistan facilitated ceasefire talks between the US and Iran. Congress leader Jairam Rames...

item.title: Omoda C5 SHS Hybrid Launches in South Africa with Advanced Features
item.description: Omoda has officially launched the new C5 SHS in South Africa, expanding its C5 compact crossover line with a hybrid option. The vehicle combines a 1.5-litre turbocharged petrol engine with a high-outp...

item.title: Kyau Forest Reserve: A Natural Fortress Sustained by Conservation and Community Efforts
item.description: Situated within Rubafu Ward of Bukoba District in Kagera Region, Kyau Forest Reserve stands as one of Tanzania’s most intriguing examples of natural conservation. Just a short thirty-minute boat ride ...

item.title: Tanzania urged to enhance fuel storage capacity to safeguard supply and economy
item.description: During a parliamentary session held in Dodoma on April 8, 2026, Ikungi West Member of Parliament Elibariki Kingu called on the Tanzanian government to invest in expanding fuel storage facilities acros...

item.title: Air Canada tests new arbitration system to speed up passenger complaint resolutions
item.description: Air Canada has announced a pilot arbitration program aimed at streamlining the resolution of passenger complaints. The project will involve 500 randomly selected customers whose compensation disputes ...

item.title: Authorities recover body from Lake District tarn while searching for missing teenager
item.description: Rescue teams have recovered the body of a teenager from a tarn in the Lake District, following a search after they were reported missing. The authorities had been conducting extensive searches in the ...

item.title: Mwalimu Nyerere Creative Writing Awards Set for April 13 in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Dar es Salaam is preparing to host the fourth edition of the National Mwalimu Nyerere Creative Writing Awards on April 13, 2026, at the SuperDome in Masaki. This was announced by Professor Adolf Mkend...

item.title: Iran proposes tolls on ships during two-week ceasefire as US and Tehran negotiate peace
item.description: Iran has agreed to a two-week ceasefire with the United States, during which the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened. According to regional officials, Tehran plans to impose tolls of up to $1 million pe...

item.title: Global tensions remain high despite Trump’s ceasefire announcement
item.description: The article reflects on the announcement of a ceasefire by former US President Donald Trump, but suggests that the world remains at risk of serious global crises. While a temporary halt in hostilities...

item.title: Trump and Iran agree on temporary two-week ceasefire amid Middle East tensions
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has agreed to a two-week ceasefire with Iran, just before his deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, averting immediate military action. Earlier, Trump had iss...

item.title: Scottie Scheffler anticipates firm and fast conditions at Augusta for Masters defence
item.description: Scottie Scheffler, the 29-year-old two-time Masters champion, returns to Augusta National this week aiming to make history as just the seventh player to win the Masters three times. Known for his cons...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Hearing Moves Forward After SC Denies TRO
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte's efforts to stop her impeachment hearing at the House of Representatives hit a roadblock after the Supreme Court denied her request for a temporary restraining order (TRO)...

item.title: Derek O’Brien challenges Election Commission over ‘get lost’ remark, demands transcript
item.description: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP Derek O’Brien has accused the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar of telling them to 'get lost' during a brief meeting. This confrontation occurred after the TM...
